notes: Casa Green is consistently listed as a leasehold condominium/apartment with the common 1,003 sqft layout; I scaled the live 1,003-sqft comparables to the target 1,001-sqft size, so the size adjustment is minimal. Live asking prices I found for similar units ranged roughly from RM440k to RM580k, while EdgeProp’s project-level 48-month data shows sold prices at RM494-RM509 psf (median RM499 psf). Brickz’s broader Bukit Jalil residential data shows a median of RM461 psf and median price of RM535k, which is in line with a market value around RM500k for this unit. I excluded rentals and room-only ads.
